articleTitle: ReCoSplat: Autoregressive Feed-Forward Gaussian Splatting Using Render-and-Compare

content.whatIsThisAbout: This paper presents a novel approach called ReCoSplat for online novel view synthesis, which aims to generate new images from different viewpoints given a sequence of input images, even without knowing the camera poses.
content.problemSolved: The paper addresses the challenge of robust scene reconstruction from sequential, often unposed, observations for online novel view synthesis.
content.keyInnovation: The key innovation is the introduction of a Render-and-Compare (ReCo) module that renders the current reconstruction from the predicted viewpoint and compares it with the incoming observation, providing a stable conditioning signal that compensates for pose errors.
howItWorks: ReCoSplat uses an autoregressive feed-forward Gaussian Splatting model to support posed or unposed inputs, with or without camera intrinsics.
The model assembles local Gaussians using camera poses, which scales better than canonical-space prediction but creates a distribution mismatch when predicted poses are used at inference.
The ReCo module addresses this issue by providing a stable conditioning signal that compensates for pose errors.

To support long sequences, the paper proposes a hybrid KV cache compression strategy combining early-layer truncation with chunk-level selective retention, reducing the KV cache size by over 90% for 100+ frames.
The combined approach of ReCoSplat and the ReCo module achieves state-of-the-art performance across different input settings on both in- and out-of-distribution benchmarks.
content.whyItMatters: This research is important because it advances the field of online novel view synthesis, which has practical applications in areas such as virtual reality, augmented reality, and 3D reconstruction. By addressing the challenge of robust scene reconstruction from sequential, unposed observations, this approach can enable more immersive and realistic experiences in these domains.
prerequisites: Basic understanding of computer vision and image processing
Knowledge of 3D reconstruction and camera pose estimation
Familiarity with deep learning and autoregressive models
Concepts related to Gaussian Splatting and render-and-compare techniques

keyTerms.definition: The process of generating new images from different viewpoints given a sequence of input images, without knowing the camera poses.
A model that assembles local Gaussians using camera poses to support posed or unposed inputs, with or without camera intrinsics.
A module that renders the current reconstruction from the predicted viewpoint and compares it with the incoming observation, providing a stable conditioning signal that compensates for pose errors.
A strategy that combines early-layer truncation with chunk-level selective retention to reduce the KV cache size by over 90% for long sequences.
The issue that arises when using ground-truth poses during training, which leads to a mismatch when predicted poses are used at inference.
The approach achieves the best results across different input settings on both in- and out-of-distribution benchmarks.

content.visualConcept: A flowchart or diagram illustrating the key components of the ReCoSplat approach, including the autoregressive feed-forward Gaussian Splatting model, the Render-and-Compare (ReCo) module, and the hybrid KV cache compression strategy. The diagram should highlight the interactions between these components and how they work together to enable online novel view synthesis.
